methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Second Front Systems is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Peter Dixon and Tyler Sweatt that has raised $90M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparable companies across the Defense & Security sector—2 public firms (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities—all at equivalent stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $90M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $171M. Peer-median analysis examines 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B—including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns—producing a median of $1.2B. The wide gap between methods reflects materially different assumptions about Second Front's relative maturity and market position within the defense-tech cohort.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $145M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $686M. This $1.25B spread reflects genuine uncertainty regarding whether Second Front tracks closer to early-stage capital deployed or to the valuation multiples of later-stage, more mature defense-tech platforms. Critical variables affecting narrower positioning include revenue traction, customer concentration, and defensibility of technology relative to named peers.
